title: Epic: Personal Event Ownership — Shift from Group-Owned to User-Owned Events
body:
## Issue description

Nova-Circle currently models events as **group-scoped entities** — every event has a mandatory `group_id` foreign key, and all event APIs are nested under `/api/v1/groups/:groupId/events`. This tightly couples event lifecycle to groups, prevents users from managing personal activities independently, and makes it impossible to share a single event across multiple groups.

This epic tracks the transformation to a **personal event ownership model** where:
- Events are owned by users, not groups
- Users manage their own calendar of personal activities and events
- Users can choose to share events to one or more groups with configurable visibility levels
- Group members can discover and view events shared to their groups
- A calendar view provides day, week, month, and custom time-frame views
- Users can filter which groups' shared events appear in their calendar

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

This epic comprises the following user stories:

1. **Personal event management** — As a user, I want to manage a calendar of personal activities and events (CRUD operations on events I own, independent of any group).
2. **Event sharing to groups** — As a user, I want to share my activities to one or more groups so that group members can see them.
3. **Configurable sharing visibility** — As a user, I want to set the level of detail shared per group (busy, title, details) so I control what others see.
4. **View group members' shared events** — As a user, I want to see events that group members have shared to the groups I belong to.
5. **Calendar view** — As a user, I want to see events in a calendar view with day, week, month, and custom time-frame modes.
6. **Calendar group filtering** — As a user, I want to filter which groups I see in the calendar view so I can focus on relevant events.

### Non-functional requirements
- Privacy-first: personal events are private by default until explicitly shared
- Security: explicit authorization checks for every shared event access
- Data minimization: shared event detail is limited by the visibility level chosen by the event owner
- Backward compatibility: existing group event workflows must continue to function during migration
- All changes must have comprehensive automated tests (unit, integration, API, authorization/privacy)

---

## Additional notes

### Architecture impact
- **Database**: The `events` table currently has a required `group_id` FK. This must become nullable or be replaced by a new `event_shares` junction table. The `event_invitations` table remains but its semantics may shift.
- **Domain model**: `Event` entity gains a user-ownership concept. A new `EventShare` entity tracks which events are shared to which groups at what visibility level.
- **API routes**: New personal event endpoints (e.g., `/api/v1/events`) alongside existing group-scoped routes. Group event listing becomes a view over shared events.
- **Event capture pipeline**: Currently requires `groupId`. Must be updated to support personal event creation (group optional) and deferred sharing.
- **Client**: Major UI changes — personal calendar view, sharing controls, visibility settings, group filters.
- **Event collaboration** (chat, checklist, location): Access model must be revisited for personal events vs. shared events.

### Related child issues
- Database schema migration for personal event ownership
- Personal event management (domain, application, API)
- Event sharing to groups with visibility levels
- View group members' shared events
- Calendar view UI (day, week, month, custom time frame)
- Calendar group filtering

### Key constraints
- Follow clean architecture (domain → application → infrastructure → presentation)
- Follow the modular monolith pattern from copilot-instructions.md
- Maintain explicit authorization — sharing does not grant edit access
- Hidden event rule still applies — unshared events must not leak existence
- All CI tests must pass

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All child issues are created and linked
- [ ] Database migration supports personal event ownership and multi-group sharing
- [ ] Users can create, read, update, and delete personal events without any group context
- [ ] Users can share events to one or more groups with visibility levels (busy, title, details)
- [ ] Users can change or revoke sharing at any time
- [ ] Group members can view events shared to their groups, respecting visibility levels
- [ ] Calendar view supports day, week, month, and custom time-frame modes
- [ ] Calendar view supports filtering by group
- [ ] Personal events not shared to any group are invisible to other users
- [ ] Event capture pipeline works for personal events (group optional)
- [ ] Comprehensive automated tests cover all new authorization and privacy rules
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass or are updated for the new model
- [ ] CI pipeline passes on all PRs
